code_symbols.role: membership test: is a query one of the frozen held-out questions
normalized sha256 identity of a held-out question
validates the suite (shapes, Mongo ground truth) and freezes hashes

store_fields: backend/evals/heldout_questions.jsonl: id, shape, question, corpora, answerable, expected_doc_ids, expected_all_docs, expected_concepts, history(optional)
rows.collision_note: Identifier drift, not concept drift: plan and critique say 56 questions ('the held-out 56', PLAN_CRITIQUE:87) but heldout_hashes.json is frozen at question_count=58 (frozen_at 2026-07-13T07:55:14Z) while all three captured EVAL_2026-07-13_*.json baselines have n=56. Two questions were added after baseline capture; any A/B against 'the 56' must subset by id or re-baseline.
rows.evidence: Code spells it 'heldout' (files/functions), plan spells it 'held-out'; both denote exactly backend/evals/heldout_questions.jsonl + its frozen hashes. Shape census on disk: direct 10 (checklist:564 says 8) confirms post-freeze additions.

code_symbols.role: the firewall: frozen-hash exclusion of held-out queries from any persisted representation
mandatory per-query gate for harvesting pipelines
precondition check: frozen hashes present before harvesting may run
normalization so reformatting cannot bypass the firewall

store_fields: backend/evals/heldout_hashes.json: hashes[] (the firewall's only store; no Mongo/Qdrant fields exist)
aliases: automated contamination eval-run check (the unbuilt half)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a): 'contamination' means two unrelated things. Here = eval-set leakage into production representations. In P0.5 it = facet metadata pollution ('corpus-lens facet contamination', CHECKLIST:118; backend/scripts/p0_5_facet_decontamination.py). Do not route P0.5 facet work through eval_firewall or vice versa. Also note: is_heldout_query has ZERO production call sites today — its only importer is freeze_heldout_eval.py:29; the checklist's automated eval-run contamination check (CHECKLIST:584-587) is deferred to P2.2 and is PLAN-ONLY.
rows.evidence: grep for eval_firewall|is_heldout_query|firewall_active across backend/*.py returns only the module itself and freeze_heldout_eval.py — the firewall exists but nothing harvest-shaped consults it yet, exactly as CHECKLIST:586 records.

code_symbols.role: harness 1: P1.1 quality eval over the heldout suite (doc_hit/recall/answerability)
harness 2: route-contract validator (issues: fail/warn codes) for the three UI tiers
harness 2's live runner over scripts/retrieval_three_tier_queries.json (5 queries)
harness 3: offline labeled-relevance metric library, deliberately not on the live path

store_fields: docs/baselines/EVAL_<date>_<tier>.json: summary{doc_hit_rate, doc_recall_mean, concept_recall_mean, answerability_ok_rate, latency_mean_s, negative_controls_ok, scorer_version, by_shape}, results[]
data_eval/ (ad-hoc probe artifacts, e.g. citation_probe_*.json, audience_pooled_relevance.json)
rows.canonical: split: 'heldout eval' (P1.1 gate) / 'three-tier eval' (route contract) / 'eval_metrics' (metric library)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (b): the plan's single word 'eval' is three disjoint code harnesses that do not share scoring code. run_heldout_eval.score() re-implements recall/hit inline and never imports eval_metrics (eval_metrics' only consumer is three_tier_eval.py:14). Concretely: P1.1 box CHECKLIST:570-571 is checked [x] claiming 'nDCG/MRR' were captured, but the heldout runner computes no nDCG/MRR and EVAL_*.json summaries contain no such keys — nDCG/MRR exist only in the un-wired eval_metrics.py. The checked box overstates what the deployed scorer measures.
rows.evidence: eval_metrics.py:1-6 docstring: 'intentionally not imported by the live retrieval path... belong in golden-set eval runs'; grep shows sole importer three_tier_eval.py:14. run_heldout_eval.py:182-211 computes its own doc_recall/concept_recall.

code_symbols.role: durable-state census baseline (Mongo/Qdrant/Neo4j counts) -> BASELINE_<date>.json
latency baseline (5 live probes incl. negative control) -> LATENCY_<date>.json
quality baseline (heldout scores per tier) -> EVAL_<date>_<tier>.json

rows.canonical: docs/baselines artifact family — qualify as census-baseline / latency-baseline / eval-baseline
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a): one word, three artifact species. In P1.1/S8 ('A/B on the held-out 56 vs the 2026-07-13 baseline', PLAN_CRITIQUE:87) 'baseline' = EVAL_*.json quality scores. In the Implementation Log ('Baseline capture (pre-edit requirement)', CHECKLIST:1272) 'baseline' = the census BASELINE_*.json + LATENCY_*.json. P1.2's acceptance 'improves materially over baseline' (CHECKLIST:611) means the EVAL numbers. A reader who fetches the wrong species will compare counts against recall rates.
rows.evidence: All three writers target docs/baselines/ with distinct filename prefixes; capture_raptor_baseline.py:14, probe_tier_latency.py:22, run_heldout_eval.py:289-291.

rows.canonical: three-tier regression matrix (to-be-built; CHECKLIST:1219-1250, 0 boxes checked per PLAN_CRITIQUE:52)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (b), high risk: matrix rows 1-12 (CHECKLIST:1224-1235: expert direct hit, naive bridge, single fact, broad, list, procedural, comparison, follow-up, negative control, cross-domain, cross-corpus, cross-corpus-irrelevant) are the heldout suite's shape taxonomy under different names (heldout shapes: direct, naive, single_fact, broad, list, procedural, comparison, followup, negative_control, cross_domain, cross_corpus, cross_corpus_irrelevant — freeze_heldout_eval.py:31-44). Only rows 13-16 (no-tree corpus, deleted corpus, cold-start, concurrent-ingest) are new. Building the matrix as a third standalone harness would triplicate scoring; the honest build is heldout shapes + 4 ops rows + evaluate_route_result's record contract. Also (a): 'regression suite' elsewhere means the ordinary pytest suite (docs/QUERY_PLAN_V2_RETRIEVAL.md:221) — not this matrix.
rows.evidence: PLAN_CRITIQUE:52 counts the matrix as 24 open / 0 done; the live three-tier query set has only 5 queries and no negative-control/forbidden_lexicon_terms row despite the validator supporting one (three_tier_eval.py:328-337).

code_symbols.role: per-question binary: any expected doc returned (ALL expected docs when expected_all_docs=true)
suite-level mean, the headline number ('Fast 84.3% doc-hit', CHECKLIST:573)
DUPLICATE: '1.0 when at least one exact gold source is recovered in top-k' — same idea, never wired to the heldout runner

rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (b): one idea implemented twice — run_heldout_eval.doc_hit and eval_metrics.exact_source_hit_at_k compute the same binary hit independently, and their semantics already diverge: doc_hit flips to ALL-expected-docs-required when the question sets expected_all_docs (run_heldout_eval.py:188-191); exact_source_hit_at_k is fixed any-hit@k with a k cutoff the heldout runner doesn't apply. Any consolidation must preserve the expected_all_docs branch or list/comparison questions will silently score easier.
rows.evidence: run_heldout_eval.py:9-14 docstring defines doc_hit; eval_metrics.py:147-158 defines the twin; no cross-reference exists between the two files.

code_symbols.role: impl 1 (answer level): shape negative_control MUST be answerable=false with no expected_doc_ids
impl 1 scorer: refusal/honest-absence detection = fail-closed credit
impl 2 (vocabulary level): fail when 'a negative-control vocabulary concept was introduced'
impl 3 (smoke level): 'Hybrid negative control — deliberately unanswerable query' (the tungsten probe)

store_fields: query-case field forbidden_lexicon_terms (three-tier query set schema; currently unused in the live 5-query set)
rows.canonical: negative_control (heldout shape) — call impl 2 'forbidden-vocabulary control' to keep them apart
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a): one phrase, two different assertions — (i) 'an unanswerable question must be refused' (heldout shape + REFUSAL_RE + probe_tier_latency) and (ii) 'this vocabulary term must never be introduced by expansion' (three_tier_eval forbidden lexicon). Also mild (b): the unanswerable-question control exists three times (heldout suite, latency probe, strict-ready smoke requirement CHECKLIST:1268) with no shared question source — the tungsten query is hardcoded in the probe, not drawn from the suite's 5 negative_control rows.
rows.evidence: three_tier_eval.py:605 literally says 'a negative-control vocabulary concept was introduced' — same words, vocabulary-level meaning; freeze_heldout_eval.py:89-90 enforces the answer-level meaning.

rows.canonical: preregistered thresholds (to-be-built; the phrase appears nowhere in the repo)
aliases: promotion gate numbers (e.g. CHECKLIST:811 'PROMOTION GATE: cosine = 0.98')
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a) adjacent: 'threshold' in code overwhelmingly means runtime production gates (ANSWERABILITY_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD, backend/services/answerability_tuning.py:97) — tuning knobs, not eval pass/fail bars; do not preregister by reusing those settings. The substantive gap: the checklist's eval acceptance criteria are qualitative ('improves materially over baseline' CHECKLIST:611, 'no held-out recall/nDCG loss' CHECKLIST:831) with no stored numbers, so today nothing is preregistered — the only pre-declared numerics anywhere are the latency budgets and the (unset) per-case min_* floors.
rows.evidence: grep -riE 'preregist' across backend/ and docs/ returns nothing; ROUTE_LATENCY_BUDGETS comment (three_tier_eval.py:44-46) is the sole 'targets declared before the run' artifact.

rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a): 'scorer' elsewhere in the codebase means production RANKING scorers — services/retriever/lexical.py:49 (fallback lexical scorer), services/retriever/evidence_allocation.py:13,66 (metadata-aware scorer), services/graph/orchestrator.py:2715 (graph ranking-signal scorer). Hygiene bug: the in-file comment still says 'scorer v2' (run_heldout_eval.py:45) while HEAD is v3 (commit e1d4a4f) and artifacts stamp scorer_version=3 — the comment is the kind of stale tag PLAN_CRITIQUE C4 warns about.
